claim_boundary: The registry defines benchmark contracts and replay rows. It does not create new hardware evidence or quantum-advantage claims.

instances.acceptance_gate: classical and exact references must report schema-compatible observable rows before simulator or hardware rows are accepted
instances.claim_boundary: Reference smoke instance for compiler and observable consistency; not a hardware-performance or advantage claim.
instances.title: Four-node Kuramoto-XY ring with linear frequency gradient

instances.acceptance_gate: exact or tensor-network reference must include runtime, dependency, and tolerance provenance before release promotion
instances.claim_boundary: Simulator/reference benchmark for scaling behaviour; not a broad quantum-advantage claim.
instances.title: Eight-node decaying-chain Kuramoto-XY benchmark

instances.acceptance_gate: raw-count replay must pass integrity checks and preserve the hardware status ledger claim boundary
instances.claim_boundary: Replays committed raw counts only; does not submit QPU jobs or promote broader scaling, mitigation, or advantage claims.
instances.coupling_model: Phase 1 DLA parity committed K matrix
instances.frequency_model: Phase 1 DLA parity committed frequency settings
instances.title: Phase 1 n=4 DLA parity hardware replay benchmark

instances.acceptance_gate: dataset schema, finite-size tolerances, and null controls must be committed before implementation status changes from planned
instances.claim_boundary: Visible roadmap row only; not an available benchmark result.
instances.title: Finite-size BKT transition grid

result_schema.hardware_rule: hardware_submission must be false unless a preregistered manifest, QPU budget, raw-count target path, and explicit approval are recorded
